source_jobs.title: Sales Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
AT KLUE https://klue.com/, WE’RE BUILDING THE FUTURE OF COMPETITIVE INTELLIGENCE




👋 KLUE SALES IS HIRING


CHECK OUT WHAT A DAY IN THE LIFE OF AN SDR IS LIKE HERE https://www.instagram.com/kluein/reel/C8s6tWhxMqF/?hl=en!

(P.s. We call our SDRs "Account Specialists" if you're having trouble finding them on LinkedIn)




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Generate Leads. You’ll build prospect lists, learn how to research accounts, cold call and warm call, craft personalized email outreach, leverage LinkedIn, and use AI to prospect at scale. You'll generate leads through:
   
   - Highly targeted outbound prospecting
   
   - Nurturing and converting inbound leads.

 - Lead introductory calls and master the art of discovery. You'll learn to identify prospect pain points and articulate Klue's value in real conversations, building this crucial sales skill through hands-on experience.

 - Have the autonomy to work on projects beyond your role. From evaluating leading edge sales technology, to improving existing processes, or learning how to interview other SDRs your impact goes beyond just prospecting.




WHAT YOU BRING

 - You have proven sales experience. It doesn’t have to be in SaaS, but you’ve consistently met and exceeded targets in your industry, demonstrating steady growth and leadership in your sales career.

 - You are relentless, and have a history of achieving. You don’t stop when you face adversity and have a proven record of growing and developing whatever you dedicate yourself to.

 - You’re self driven. No one has to tell you to be hungry to achieve. We’ll help point you in the right direction but it’s on you to push to get there and that comes naturally.

 - You are curious and keen to learn. You’ll keep digging until you find the answer and when you do you’ll question it in case you can improve what you dig up.

 - You’re a tech-savvy early adopter. You’re willing to get uncomfortable testing new things in the pursuit of maximizing your outcomes. You’re also the first to share what’s working because you know it’ll elevate the team




FOUNDATIONAL SDR PROGRAM

This is a learning culture. Your growth matters here. Starting with our 2-week foundational SDR onboarding program where you'll build strong prospecting skills with continued training throughout your 3-month ramp. Our team will help you advance, and in time, you'll mentor newer SDRs. Our commitment is clear: prepare you to be an expert prospector and a well-rounded sales professional.



⬇️ ⬇️ ⬇️ ⬇️ ⬇️ ⬇️ ⬇️ ⬇️ ⬇️ ⬇️ ⬇️ ⬇️ ⬇️ ⬇️ ⬇️ ⬇️ ⬇️

Not ticking every box? That’s okay. We take potential into consideration. An equivalent combination of education and experience may be accepted in lieu of the specifics listed above. If you know you have what it takes, even if that’s different from what we’ve described, be sure to explain why in your application.

At Klue, we're committed to building a high-performing team where people feel a strong sense of belonging, can be their authentic selves, and are able to reach their full potential. If there’s anything we can do to make our hiring process more accessible or to better support you, please let us know, we’re happy to accommodate.







WHY KLUE MIGHT BE THE PERFECT FIT FOR YOU

At Klue, you’ll push the boundaries of what’s possible. For yourself, your team, and our customers. The environment here feels more startup than corporate. You’ll roll up your sleeves, build, iterate, and level up. This is a place where your ideas matter, you can shape what comes next, and leave your mark.

If you’re looking to join a category-defining company… where you own big outcomes, influence real customers, and work with smart, gritty people… you’ll thrive here.



Unlock Your Potential. Take Risks. Take Action. Innovate




HOW WE WORK AT KLUE

We love the balance of connection and flexibility. We work Office-First, with a hybrid touch, and work together in our vibrant offices on Mondays, Wednesdays & Thursdays to collaborate, brainstorm, and bui

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
AT KLUE https://klue.com/, WE’RE BUILDING THE FUTURE OF COMPETITIVE INTELLIGENCE


👋 KLUE PRODUCT IS HIRING!




We’re looking for a Senior Product Designer who can infuse a human centric visually striking experience in an agentic paradigm. You’re highly polished in your craft and building a compelling user experience is at the core of what you do. Every pixel matters to you.



This is an opportunity to build a brand new product from the ground up. There is no playbook to follow and you have to come up with novel ways to expose the complexity of agents in a way that feels functional, fluid, and emotionally connecting.



If delivering that type of experience with craft, simplicity and speed interests you... let's talk.




WHAT’S IN IT FOR YOU?

You will get to work on a brand new AI product at a startup - not bolting AI to an existing product but to rethink how competitive and buyer intelligence is done in the AI era. You’ll work with a team of researchers, ML specialists and builders that are serious about experimentation, iteration, and craft, with real latitude to push on AI experiences.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Owner: Own the end-to-end experience of the new product.

 - Craftsmenship: Bring genuine aesthetic judgment to everything you touch. Your screens are polished, considered, and consistent. You have opinions about typography, spacing, and color, and you act on them.

 - Builder: You don’t only define the design but you check-in the code and ship directly to customers.

 - AI-pilled: Bring your existing AI process and tools to uplevel the team and develop new ones. You look at AI as a force multiplier 10xing your outcomes.




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

You’ll know you’re thriving when:

 - Customers love your experience.

 - You’re shipping significant new value to customers every sprint.

 - You crush the success metrics.




WHAT YOU BRING


MUST-HAVES:

 - Experience: Experience designing and shipping beautiful products used by thousands of users.

 - Visual Taste: Your portfolio makes it obvious that you have solved some really hard design challenges in an aesthetically pleasing way.

 - AI Tool Fluency: You can clearly demonstrate that you are not the average AI tool user. You're pushing the boundaries of what the AI tools can offer.

 - Drive for outcomes: You can demonstrate that your work has had a meaningful impact on the company’s outcomes.

 - Communication: You are an effective communicator.

⬇️ ⬇️ ⬇️ ⬇️ ⬇️ ⬇️ ⬇️ ⬇️ ⬇️ ⬇️ ⬇️ ⬇️ ⬇️ ⬇️ ⬇️ ⬇️ ⬇️

Not ticking every box? That’s okay. We take potential into consideration. An equivalent combination of education and experience may be accepted in lieu of the specifics listed above. If you know you have what it takes, even if that’s different from what we’ve described, be sure to explain why in your application.

At Klue, we're committed to building a high-performing team where people feel a strong sense of belonging, can be their authentic selves, and are able to reach their full potential. If there’s anything we can do to make our hiring process more accessible or to better support you, please let us know, we’re happy to accommodate.







WHY KLUE MIGHT BE THE PERFECT FIT FOR YOU

At Klue, you’ll push the boundaries of what’s possible. For yourself, your team, and our customers. The environment here feels more startup than corporate. You’ll roll up your sleeves, build, iterate, and level up. This is a place where your ideas matter, you can shape what comes next, and leave your mark.

If you’re looking to join a category-defining company… where you own big outcomes, influence real customers, and work with smart, gritty people… you’ll thrive here.



Unlock Your Potential. Take Risks. Take Action. Innovate




HOW WE WORK AT KLUE

We love the balance of connection and flexibility. We work Office-First, with a hybrid touch, and work together in our vibrant offices on Mondays, Wednesdays & Thursdays to collaborate, brainstorm, and build together.

Our main hiring hub

source_jobs.title: Social & Video Creator
source_jobs.description:
AT KLUE https://klue.com/, WE’RE BUILDING THE FUTURE OF COMPETITIVE INTELLIGENCE


👋 KLUE MARKETING IS HIRING



As a Social & Video Creator, you'll operate at the intersection of brand storytelling, supporting social content strategy, and video production. You'll use Klue's studio, channels, and creative direction to build content that grows an owned audience of product marketers and competitive intelligence practitioners, and makes Klue the B2B brand other marketers study.



Your mission? To produce creative content that builds Klue's social presence, earns audience loyalty, and gives the marketing team a consistent, high-quality output to build on. You'll shoot, edit, produce, and distribute: managing the day-to-day content engine and leading the big-swing campaigns that define how Klue shows up in the market.



When our audience grows, when practitioners tell us Klue's content is the sharpest in the space, when a series drives real pipeline, that's when you're winning.




WHAT’S IN IT FOR YOU?

You'll work hands-on with a full creative studio and real production budget, building content that actually reaches relevant audiences. You'll develop deep fluency in B2B brand building, audience development, and video production for niche but highly engaged markets.



You'll see direct impact on brand and pipeline, while working closely with Klue's marketing leadership and getting genuine visibility into how a category-defining company builds its story.



As you excel, there's room to own more; expanded scope, higher-impact projects, and creative leadership opportunities.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own and execute the weekly social content calendar across LinkedIn and YouTube, producing short-form video, clips, and creative posts that grow Klue's following.

 - Film and edit content independently using Klue's studio setup, as well as lo-fi phone formats.

 - Lead production of major creative projects (docuseries, ad series, creative campaigns) from concept through publish.

 - Manage shoot logistics, b-roll capture, scripting, story editing, and the full production pipeline.

 - Coordinate with external video editors, animators, and contractors to ship on time and on quality.

 - Extract maximum distribution from every project (clips, serialized drops, social pulls, repurposed cuts).

 - Pitch original series and format concepts grounded in Klue's positioning and audience interests.

 - Track channel performance and bring data-backed recommendations on what to double down on.

 - Produce video-based customer stories, ad creative, and supporting assets for the marketing flywheel.




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

We want you to love the role you are in and believe these skills and experiences will help you to be successful! You’ll know you’re successful when:

 - Klue's LinkedIn and YouTube channels are growing measurably, with a publishing cadence that never goes dark.

 - Major campaigns ship on time with clear distribution plans and generate content that gets talked about.

 - You're bringing series ideas to the table before being asked and some of them get made.

 - The team has a library of on-brand, high-quality video assets that get used across channels, not just filed away.

 - Other B2B marketers cite Klue's content when they talk about brands worth emulating.




WHAT YOU BRING

Must Haves:

 - Practical experience shooting professional-grade video and editing. Understands story structure, pacing, and b-roll; comfortable in a studio and with lo-fi formats.

 - Genuine social-native instincts. Opinions about what performs on LinkedIn and YouTube and can back them up with results.

 - Production pipeline discipline. Can manage a content calendar, coordinate contractors, and ship consistently without being managed.

 - Creative concept muscle. Goes from brief to concrete pitch; brings specific ideas, not generic availability.

 - Strong creative eye. Framing, visual quality, pacing, and brand consistency across formats.



Nice-t

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer, AI
source_jobs.description:
AT KLUE https://klue.com/, WE’RE BUILDING THE FUTURE OF COMPETITIVE INTELLIGENCE




👋 KLUE ENGINEERING IS HIRING!

We're looking for a Senior Software Engineer to join our team in Toronto, focusing on building and optimizing state-of-the-art LLM-powered agents that can reason, plan and automate workflows for users. You will be leading the design and development of search and retrieval agent systems that enable users to generate compete insights for their business. In this role, you will own projects end-to-end, guiding architecture decisions, experimentation strategy, and production readiness for LLM-powered retrieval and generation workflows.




You will shape how we integrate retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), dense retrieval, query understanding, and agentic reasoning loops to deliver fast, accurate, and trusted search experiences at scale.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build and ship backend systems that power agentic workflows. You design retrieval pipelines, orchestration layers, and multi-step agent architectures that turn millions of competitive data points (news, press releases, webpage changes, Slack posts, emails, reviews, CRM data) into actionable intelligence for our customers.

 - Own evaluation of agentic systems at scale. You develop and operate evaluation frameworks (automated, offline, and human-in-the-loop) that measure relevance, quality, latency, and end-to-end task success across our agent pipelines. You'll define what "good" looks like and build the infrastructure to measure it continuously.

 - Design and optimize retrieval and ranking systems. You work across hybrid retrieval, re-ranking, query rewriting, and post-retrieval synthesis to ensure our agents surface the right information at the right time. You understand the tradeoffs between BM25, dense retrieval, and hybrid approaches and know when each matters.

 - Improve LLM-powered workflows end to end. From prompt design and retrieval strategy to caching and latency optimization, you'll make our agent responses faster, more accurate, and more reliable in production.

 - Ship with the customer in mind. You connect technical decisions to customer outcomes. You're energized by understanding how customers use the product, and you use that context to prioritize what to build next. You ship iteratively, measure impact, and course-correct quickly.

 - Collaborate across product, infrastructure, and data teams — align technical direction with product goals, contribute to architecture decisions, and help the team move faster by establishing patterns and best practices for production-grade agentic systems.

 - Stay on the frontier. Evaluate and integrate advances in LLMs, retrieval architectures, and agentic reasoning. You have strong opinions (loosely held) about where this space is heading and bring that perspective to your work.




WHAT YOU BRING

 - Experience building and operating backend systems in production, with meaningful experience in at least one of: search/retrieval, data pipelines, distributed systems, or API-heavy service architectures.

 - Hands-on experience with search, retrieval, or ranking systems. You've built or significantly improved retrieval pipelines and understand information retrieval fundamentals (hybrid retrieval, relevance tuning, query understanding).

 - Experience building or evaluating agentic / LLM-powered systems. You've worked with retrieval-augmented generation, multi-step agent workflows, or similar architectures and have thought critically about how to evaluate their output quality at scale.

 - Strong software engineering fundamentals. You write clean, maintainable, well-tested code. You're comfortable with Python and have experience with backend frameworks, APIs, and production infrastructure. You care about reliability, observability, and CI/CD.

 - Familiarity with vector databases and search infrastructure. You've worked with tools like FAISS, PGVector, Pinecone, Weaviate, Elasticsearch, or OpenSearch and understa

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer II, AI
source_jobs.description:
AT KLUE https://klue.com/, WE’RE BUILDING THE FUTURE OF COMPETITIVE INTELLIGENCE




👋 KLUE ENGINEERING IS HIRING!

We're looking for a Software Engineer, AI to join our team in Toronto, focusing on building and optimizing state-of-the-art LLM-powered agents that can reason, plan and automate workflows for users. You'll be joining us at an exciting time as we reinvent our insight generation systems, making this an excellent opportunity for someone with strong Backend and ML fundamentals who wants to dive deep into practical LLM applications.




As a member of our team, you'll be leading the design and implementation of search and retrieval agent systems that enable users to discover high-quality, relevant information with minimal effort. You will work at the intersection of LLM-powered agent workflows, retrieval pipelines, and evaluation frameworks, ensuring that our systems remain scalable, efficient, and aligned with user intent.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design and implement retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems with agentic workflows to refine query understanding, document retrieval, and response synthesis.

 - Build and optimize retrieval pipelines using BM25, dense retrieval, hybrid retrieval, and re-ranking approaches.

 - Develop evaluation pipelines for retrieval and generation, including offline metrics (recall, MRR, nDCG) and human-in-the-loop evaluations.

 - Experiment with query rewriting, expansion, and classification to improve retrieval relevance.

 - Collaborate closely with Product to bring ML-powered search agents into production.

 - Profile, debug, and optimize the latency, accuracy, and scalability of retrieval and generation components.

 - Contribute to the design of data pipelines for training retrieval and ranking models, including dataset curation, augmentation, and labeling workflows.

 - Stay up-to-date with advancements in LLMs, retrieval techniques, and agent architectures, evaluating opportunities to integrate them into our systems.




WHAT YOU BRING

 - Software engineering experience

 - Experience with information retrieval systems, search relevance, and ranking models

 - Expertise in Python, with experience in frameworks such as PyTorch, TensorFlow, or JAX.

 - Familiarity with LLMs, prompt engineering, and retrieval-augmented generation pipelines.

 - Understanding of evaluation methods for search systems, including offline metrics and user-facing evaluation.

 - Experience working with vector database infrastructure (FAISS, Milvus, Weaviate, Pinecone, PGVector) and traditional search engines (Elasticsearch, OpenSearch)

 - Understanding of data pipelines, preprocessing, and large-scale data handling.

 - Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment, balancing research and production needs.

 - Develop and implement CI/CD pipelines. Automate the deployment and monitoring of ML models.

 - Knowledge of query understanding, document summarization and other content enrichment strategies

 - Expertise in automated LLM evaluation, including LLM-as-judge methodologies

 - Skilled at prompt engineering - including zero-shot, few-shot, and chain-of-thought.

 - Experience with cloud infrastructure (AWS, GCP, Azure) for scalable ML workflows.

Nice to Have

 - Experience with agentic system design for LLM workflows.

 - Background in conversational search.

 - Contributions to open-source projects in the retrieval, NLP, or LLM ecosystems.
   




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

We're looking for builders who:

 - Take ownership and run with ambiguous problems

 - Jump into new areas and rapidly learn what's needed to deliver solutions

 - Bring scientific rigor while maintaining a pragmatic delivery focus

 - See unclear requirements as an opportunity to shape the solution




OUR TECH STACK

 - LLM platforms: OpenAI, Anthropic, open-source models

 - ML frameworks: PyTorch, Transformers, spaCy

 - Search/Vector DBs: Elasticsearch, Pinecone, PostgreSQL

 - MLOps too

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer, Agents
source_jobs.description:

👋 KLUE ENGINEERING IS HIRING!



We're looking for a Senior Software Engineer to join our team in Vancouver, someone excited to build and optimize state-of-the-art LLM-powered agents at scale. You'll bring a builder's mindset, scientific rigor, and relentless customer focus. You'll have an outsized impact on the product while staying close to the frontier of AI.



You will shape how we build and operate AI agents at scale, from multi-agent orchestration and sub-agent design to the eval frameworks that keep outputs trusted and measurable. This means optimizing across the full stack: inference costs at scale, retrieval and query performance, and the feedback loops that make agents genuinely improve over time.



You won't just execute on the roadmap, you'll help shape it, bringing a technical point of view on where the product should go and working closely with product leadership to get there. In this role, you will own projects end-to-end, guiding architecture decisions, experimentation strategy, and production readiness for our LLM-powered agents.

You design retrieval pipelines, orchestration layers, and multi-step agent architectures that turn millions of competitive data points (news, press releases, webpage changes, Slack posts, emails, reviews, CRM data) into actionable intelligence for our customers.

You develop and operate evaluation frameworks (automated, offline, and human-in-the-loop) that measure relevance, quality, latency, and end-to-end task success across our agent pipelines. source_jobs.title: Program Management Associate, Intern
source_jobs.description:
AT KLUE https://klue.com/, WE’RE BUILDING THE FUTURE OF COMPETITIVE INTELLIGENCE




THE WIN-LOSS TEAM IS HIRING 👋




WHAT IS THE WIN-LOSS TEAM?

At Klue, our team of experienced analysts design and deliver research programs for a wide range of business-to-business (B2B) technology companies. Using a structured, scalable process, we enable our clients to improve their win rates by making more informed and impactful business decisions.



Klue acquired https://techcouver.com/2025/03/18/klue-acquires-goldpan-to-advance-ai-driven-win-loss-research/Goldpan.ai http://Goldpan.ai in 2025, enhancing our product offering. With this acquisition, we are continuing to add invaluable win-loss data to enrich the roles of our ever-growing client-base. We are excited about what the future holds ahead for us and our clients!



As a Program Management Associate, Intern, you'll operate at the intersection of win-loss research, B2B technology programs, and qualitative research recruitment operations. You'll leverage tools like HubSpot, Nooks, LinkedIn, and AI-assisted workflows to build outreach campaigns and recruitment processes that transform contact data and stale CRM data into completed, high-quality research interviews.



Your mission? To recruit qualified participants for win-loss research programs that B2B technology companies rely on to make more informed, impactful business decisions. You'll champion outreach quality, partner closely with CSMs and Researchers, and influence how programs run — helping shape how Klue delivers win-loss research at scale. When clients get the interviews they need, the Win-Loss team succeeds too.




WHAT'S IN IT FOR YOU?

You'll work hands-on with multi-channel outreach, program management, and AI-forward workflows, executing recruitment processes that directly impact research quality and client outcomes. You'll develop expertise in win-loss interview recruitment, becoming the internal expert on outreach strategy across Klue's programs.



You'll see a direct impact on interview utilization and client retention while strengthening your ability to run complex, high-volume research programs with precision. Working closely with CSMs, Researchers, AEs, and AI Ops, you'll play a meaningful role in shaping how Klue's Win-Loss team delivers research programs at scale.



If you excel in this role, future growth opportunities down the road include, deepening your expertise within the PMA function or moving into qualitative research, customer success, or outbound sales.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Interview Recruitment and Outreach. Execute high-volume, multi-channel outreach via email, phone (parallel dialing via Nooks), and LinkedIn to recruit qualified participants for win-loss research interviews. Manage outreach across ~30 active customer programs simultaneously, track and update recruitment statuses in HubSpot accurately and on time, identify when programs are stalling, investigate root causes, and proactively propose solutions. Test new outreach tactics and contribute ideas to improve interview conversion rates across the team.

 - Interview Qualification and Handoff. Ensure interview opportunity data is clean, structured, and qualified before research calls. Proactively fill in missing details on HubSpot interview cards and communicate with researchers about anything that could affect interview quality or research scope alignment — so researchers are never walking into a call underprepared.

 - Internal Collaboration and Account Health. Serve as a connected, communicative member of each program's account team alongside the CSM and Researcher. Regularly share recruitment status, challenges, and proposed solutions so the team is never caught off guard. Contribute to account health discussions, flag risks to program momentum or renewal, and join customer calls when needed.

 - Recruitment Expertise and Continuous Improvement. Build fluency in Klue's outreach approaches (Sales First, Leadership First, Klue Direct)

source_jobs.title: Customer Success Manager
source_jobs.description:
AT KLUE https://klue.com/, WE’RE BUILDING THE FUTURE OF COMPETITIVE INTELLIGENCE




THE CUSTOMER SUCCESS TEAM IS HIRING 👋

You're the person who walks into an account and immediately sees what matters: the risk no one's named yet, the expansion hiding in plain sight, the champion waiting to be developed. You earn trust fast, ask the right questions, and turn a customer's goals into a plan that actually sticks.



If that sounds like you, keep reading. We'd love to meet you.



As a Customer Success Manager at Klue, you own the full customer relationship, from onboarding through renewal and expansion. You're the connective tissue: keeping every account team aligned, every customer growing, and every risk on the radar before it becomes a problem. Klue's product moves fast. New AI agents, integrations, and capabilities ship continuously. The CSMs who thrive here stay ahead of it, act on signals early, and show up as both a trusted advisor and a confident commercial partner.



Your Mission? To make sure every customer in your book gets real, measurable value from Klue, and grows because of it. You build the relationships, drive the adoption, and close the commercial outcomes. When your customers win, you win.




WHAT'S IN IT FOR YOU?

This role puts you at the center of how B2B companies use competitive intelligence to win deals, grow revenue, and own their markets. You'll work across industries and stakeholder levels, build deep customer intuition, and gain hard-won experience running complex change management inside leading organizations. In an AI-first company shipping continuously, your expertise compounds fast. No two years here look the same.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own Renewals and Expansions. Your book, your number. You forecast it, manage it, and hit it. That means spotting churn signals before anyone else names them, building the business case for expansion, and running cross-sell motions with Sales, including Klue's win-loss interview program. The commercial outcomes are yours.

 - Drive Adoption and Platform Completeness. Klue ships fast. Your job is to make sure every customer keeps up: AI agents running, integrations live, Curators and Consumers actually using the platform. You stay current on what's new and make it land, translating product velocity into conversations your customers care about.

 - Map Outcomes and Demonstrate ROI. You connect Klue to what your customers actually care about: win rate, rep confidence, PMM efficiency. You quantify it, document it, and make it impossible to argue with at renewal time. ROI isn't a slide you build at the end, it's a story you're telling all year.

 - Multi-Thread and Lead Change. Single-threaded accounts are at-risk accounts. You build across levels by default: economic buyers, Champions, Consumers, exec sponsors. You help customers navigate their own organizations and drive adoption through real friction, not around it.

 - Lead the Account Team. You own the account, which means owning the strategy and everyone working inside it. Win-Loss Researchers, PMAs, Sales, AI Solutions. You set the direction. They execute alongside you.




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

We want you to love the role you're in and believe these are the things that matter most. You'll know you're nailing it when:

 - GRR and expansion targets are met or exceeded for your book every quarter.

 - All eligible customers have their AI agents running: right competitors tracked, right integrations live, platform usage above benchmarks.

 - Upsell and cross-sell opportunities are identified, qualified, and closed at or above target. Win-loss cross-sell pipeline exists and is actively moving for eligible accounts.

 - Every account has at least one documented ROI moment per quarter, tied to a specific Klue outcome.

 - Every account has at least two active contacts at different levels. No single-threaded accounts in your book.

 - Customers are activated on new product capabilities within one quarter of relea

source_jobs.title: Research Manager
source_jobs.description:
AT KLUE https://klue.com/, WE’RE BUILDING THE FUTURE OF COMPETITIVE INTELLIGENCE




👋 KLUE WIN-LOSS IS HIRING!

As a Research Manager, you'll be the person our customers and internal teams rely on to uncover the why behind every win, loss, and churn, and then scale that capability through AI. Your mission is twofold: deliver high-impact research programs that sharpen our customers' go-to-market strategies, and actively train, tune, and supervise the AI interviewers that let us do this at scale.

When our customers win because of insights you uncovered, and the AI you trained, we all win.




WHAT’S IN IT FOR YOU?

You'll get a front-row seat to the future of qualitative research: a model where expert researchers focus their time on high-trust, high-signal conversations AI can't replicate — while continuously improving the AI interviewers that handle the rest. You'll master interviewing craft, qualitative analysis, and AI system training, all within a team actively reshaping how win-loss research gets done. This role offers a clear path to Senior Research Manager, Research Director, or AI-enabled research leadership within a high-growth, high-trust team.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Research Program Leadership. Own program delivery end-to-end: customer onboarding, interview strategy (human and AI mix), analysis, and reporting. Ensure every program delivers actionable insights that drive renewal and expansion.

 - High-Signal Interviewing. Conduct win, loss, churn, and seller interviews where human judgment is essential: high-trust relationships, complex buyer situations, and conversations that unlock insights AI can't yet reach on its own.

 - Train and Tune AI Interviewers. Design, train, and continuously improve AI interviewers using real interview data, feedback loops, and your expert judgment. A growing share of eligible interviews will be handled by AI, and you'll own the quality bar for that output.

 - Insight and Analysis. Transform interview findings into compelling research briefs, executive summaries, and narratives customers can act on, delivered inside the Klue platform, including Ask Klue and agentic outputs powered by win-loss data.

 - Customer Partnership. Build trusted relationships with customer program stakeholders. Manage expectations, surface expansion opportunities, and partner with CSMs to support renewal outcomes.

 - Continuous Improvement. Identify workflow improvements and automation opportunities. Contribute to evolving Klue's research practice as the human/AI interview model matures.




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

We want you to love the role you’re in, and we believe these outcomes will set you up for success:

 - Program execution. ≥80% on-target interview and report delivery across 100% of assigned programs each quarter.

 - AI interview growth. A growing percentage of eligible interviews completed by AI interviewer quarter-over-quarter, without sacrificing insight quality or customer satisfaction.

 - AI quality. AI-led interviews meet defined quality standards and require fewer human interventions over time, a direct reflection of your training and tuning.

 - Customer outcomes. Research programs contribute to renewal and expansion targets through consistent interview volume and insight quality.

 - Human leverage. Human-led interviews are intentionally reserved for high-trust, high-signal conversations that demonstrably influence program outcomes and customer confidence.




WHAT YOU BRING

Must Haves:

 - Strong communication skills across interviewing, writing, and customer-facing presentations.

 - Excellent qualitative research skills: probing, active listening, sense-making, and narrative development.

 - Ability to design, train, and continuously improve AI interviewers using real interviews, feedback loops, and expert judgment.

 - Strong organizational and program management skills across multiple concurrent customer engagements.
